questions.question_text: Lokmanya spoke more eloquently from the Mandalay fortress than through the columns of the printed Kesari. What is the meaning of 'eloquently' in the above sentence?
options: Language understood by the uneducated mass

questions.question_text: Which of the following is the result of Lokmanya Tilak's exemplary life?
options: People resolved to fulfil his life's dreams
The newspapers edited by him did not incur money loss
The books written by him were useful
Critics still find fault with his views

questions.question_text: In the context of the passage, which of the following statement about Lokmanya Tilak and Mahatma Gandhi is true?
options: Their influence on people was negligible
Very few people used to read the newspapers edited by them
They were moral in private life but lax in public life
They were allowed to edit their newspapers even from inside the jail

questions.question_text: Which of the following is the general tendency of critics, according to the passage?
options: To find fault with one or the other expression of a writer
To price only those writers whom they like
To suggest new ideas to the public

questions.question_text: Lokmanya Tilak's messages were most effective:
options: when he delivered through his editorials

questions.question_text: Fill in the blanks with appropriate prepositions: Rennet casein is produced ___ skim milk by coagulation ___ rennet. Soluble calcium ions are ___ this reaction. Sufficient quantities ___ calcium is already present in milk.
Match List I with List II. List I: (A) Plagiarize (B) Dubious (C) Contemptuous (D) Hamper. List II: (I) Disrespectful (II) Gift (III) Uncertain (IV) Copy.
Select the suitable grammatically correct sentence from the given options.
options: The team is confident to win the match
The team is confident of winning the match
The team is confident of win the match
The team is confident for winning the match

questions.question_text: Fill in the blank with appropriate word: The foodgrains stocks in India are at all time ___
Choose the correct spelling word for: "A board game in which players buildup words from small size lettered tiles."
If 5x + 4y = 83 and 2x/3y = 1 1/21, then the value of x^2 - y^2 is:
Find the greatest possible length which can be used to measure exactly the lengths 16 m 65 cm, 9 m and 4 m 95 cm.
The average of marks of 15 papers is 42. The average of marks of first 8 papers is 45 and that of the last 8 papers is 36. The marks of 8th paper are:
10 years ago, the ratio of ages of Vikas and Amish was 1:3. If the ratio of their present ages is 3:5, then after five years the ratio of their ages will be:

Sonu and Monu undertake to do a piece of work for 3,600. Sonu alone can do it in 6 days while Monu alone can do it in 8 days. With the help of Ramu, they finish it in 3 days. The share of Ramu will be:

There are 40 girls in a row. Muskan shifted her position 5 places to her left so that there are 10 girls to her left in the row. If Nisha was 4 places right to Muskan's original position, what is the Nisha's position from the Muskan's left side row end?
If in a certain code, 15789 is written as EGKPT and 2436 is written as ALUR, how is 24539 written in that code?
Statements: (i) All leaves are dim. (ii) No dim is brush. (iii) All tops are brushes. Conclusions: I. Some tops are leaves. II. Some dim is top. III. Some dim is leaf. IV. Some tops are brushes.
Identify the correct set for the word FAITH based on the given matrices.
Select the pair that has the same relationship as the original pair of words given below : Egg: Omelette
Which article of the Indian constitution provides for the institution of Panchayati Raj?

Who was the first Indian women to win gold in Asian games?
Assertion A: Radio waves bend in a magnetic field. Reason R: Radio waves are electromagnetic in nature.
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is not false
A is not false but R is true

questions.question_text: Statement I: Dolamite is used as refractory material and flux in iron and steel industry. Statement II: Lignite and low grade bituminous coal were formed during the quaternary period.
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct

List II: (I) Non-Rival in consumption and excludable (II) Rival in consumption and excludable (III) Non rival in consumption and Non-excludable (IV) Rival in consumption and Non-excludable

Which of the following statements are correct? A. Life cycle hypothesis was propounded by Francois Modigliani B. Absolute Income Hypothesis was propounded by Milton Friedman C. Relative Income Hypothesis was propounded by James Duesenberry. D. Permanent Income Hypothesis was propounded by John Maynard Keynes

Assertion A: Pareto optimality implies that an allocation of resources is inefficient when it is possible... to benefit at least one person without making anyone else worse off. Reason R: Pareto efficiency takes into account distributive justice and compensation of people affected negatively.
options: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are correct but R is NOT the correct explanation of A.
A is correct but R is not correct.
A is not correct but R is correct.

options: Continue to operate as it is

options: A decline in marginal physical product

options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct

questions.question_text: Statement I: Coefficient of correlation can not be less than - 1. Statement II: Regression coefficient can not be less than - 1.
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true

options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false

questions.question_text: The Garran Tribunal was constituted during the colonial times to:
options: discuss about the division of taxes between the provinces and centre in colonial India
discuss and decide about the division of financial responsibilities between India and Britain on military expenditure.
decide on the pricing of railway tariffs in colonial India
decide about the famine relief strategy in the background of the Great Bengal Famine

options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.question_text: Suppose massive capital outflows cause nominal exchange rate to depreciate by 10%. At the same time, foreign and domestic price levels rise by 5% and 20% respectively. What will be the approximate change in real exchange rate?

options: X good then bad: Y good
Y good then bad: X good

options: log x + log y = c1 + c2

options: lambda f(x1) + (1-lambda) f(x2) > f[lambda x1 + (1-lambda) x2]
lambda f(x1) + (1-lambda) f(x2) < f[lambda x1 + (1-lambda) x2]
lambda f(x1) + (1-lambda) f(x2) >= f[lambda x1 + (1-lambda) x2]

questions.question_text: Linear regression model is:
options: Linear in explanatory variables but may not be linear in parameters
Nonlinear in parameters and must be linear in variables
Linear in parameters and must be linear in variables

the actual and estimated X values

options: The money multiplier increases from 0.25 to 2.8
The money multiplier increases from 0.25 to 1.25
The money multiplier falls from 4 to 2.8
The money multiplier increases from 2.8 to 4

the sum of the elasticities of exports and imports should be less than 1
the sum of the elasticities of exports and imports should be equal to 1
the sum of the elasticities of exports and imports should be equal to zero

questions.question_text: Statement I: In case of inferior goods, Engel curve is upward sloping. Statement II: In case of normal goods, Engel curve is downward sloping.
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false

questions.question_text: Arrange the following former Governors of RBI in the chronological order... A. C. Rangaswami (likely C. Rangarajan) B. D. Subbarao C. I. G. Patel D. Y. V. Reddy E. Bimal Jalan

options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true

questions.question_text: Assertion A: For matrix A=[[1,2],[8,16]], rank of A=1. Reason R: For matrix A if |A|=0, then it implies that rank of matrix A is less than full rank.
options: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are correct but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is correct but R is not correct
A is not correct but R is correct

questions.question_text: Assertion A: General equilibrium is attained in an economy when demand equals supply in all markets simultaneously. Reason R: In a general equilibrium model, relative prices cannot be determined.
options: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are correct but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is correct but R is not correct
A is not correct but R is correct

questions.question_text: Assertion A: Let vector a=(x,y,z) and vector b=(2,3,5). Then a=b if and only if x=2, y=3 and z=5. Reason R: Two vectors are equal if all their corresponding components are equal.
options: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are correct but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is correct but R is not correct
A is not correct but R is correct

questions.question_text: Assertion A: According to the Heckscher-Ohlin model, free trade results in equalisation of factor prices across nations. Reason R: This is because the model assumes perfect international mobility of the factors of production.
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true

questions.question_text: Match List I (Theory) with List II (Economist). (A) Neoclassical investment (B) q theory (C) MEC (D) Multiplier-accelerator. (I) Keynes (II) Jorgenson (III) Samuelson (IV) Tobin.

options: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are correct but R is NOT the correct explanation of A

options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false

questions.question_text: Statement I: When the economy is in liquidity trap, the LM curve is vertical. Statement II: Monetary policy is ineffective when the economy is in liquidity trap.
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.question_text: Assertion A: If a proposition P implies proposition Q, then we can say that P is a sufficient condition for Q... Reason R: For proportion Q to be true it is sufficient that P is true i.e.. Q is a necessary condition for P.
options: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are correct but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
